name: Buckwheat Crepes with Potato and Mushroom Filling
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Portobello mushrooms or shiitake mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the mushrooms and cut them into 1/2-inch dice.
2. Heat a tablespoon of olive oil and a teaspoon of butter in a large non-stick skillet. Stir in the minced garlic for about a minute.
3. Add the mushrooms and a little salt, then sauté for 10-12 minutes until tender and starting to sizzle.
4. In another skillet, cook the chopped onions in the remaining olive oil and butter with a dash of salt until soft and turning golden.
5. Peel the potatoes and cut into 1/2-inch dice. Combine with white wine and enough water to barely cover them in a saucepan, plus 1/2 teaspoon of salt.
6. Bring to a boil, lower heat to a simmer, cover tightly, and cook for 10-15 minutes until tender. Remove the lid and continue cooking gently, stirring once or twice, until the liquid thickens into a sauce.
7. Stir in the sautéed onions and mushrooms, parsley, and fresh-ground black pepper to taste.
8. Allow the potato-mushroom mixture to cool slightly. Fill each crepe with 2-3 rounded tablespoons of filling, then sprinkle a little grated cheese over the filling. Roll or fold the crepe loosely around it.
9. Cover the crepes loosely with aluminum foil and bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 10-15 minutes until hot through and the cheese is completely melted.
